metadata.name: Server Disk Usage Monitor
metadata.description: Standalone Python monitor that checks local disk usage for one path and sends a Discord warning when usage reaches a configurable threshold.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
This template renders a standalone `main.py` script plus `requirements.txt` for disk usage monitoring on the machine where the script runs.

## What It Does
- Runs `df -P` locally for one configured path, such as `/`.
- Compares the usage percentage to a configurable warning threshold.
- Sends a Discord webhook warning only when the threshold is reached or exceeded.
- Prints a plain stdout summary for every run.

## Prerequisites
- The machine running the script must have the `df` command available.
- If you want notifications, configure the Discord webhook URL.

## Run
1. Render `main.py` and `requirements.txt`.
2. Review the monitored path, threshold slider, and Discord settings in the rendered file.
3. Run `python3 main.py` directly or from cron/systemd on the server you want to monitor.

## Notes
- The warning threshold is configurable with a slider in the template UI.
- The script checks the local machine only; there is no SSH target or authentication setup.
- The script exits successfully after a completed check, even when disk usage is above the warning threshold.
- Only actual execution problems such as invalid config, missing `df`, or webhook errors cause a non-zero exit.

## References
- [Discord Webhooks](https://docs.discord.com/developers/resources/webhook)
- [df command](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man1/df.1.html)

variables.name: warning_percent
variables.title: Warning Threshold Percent
variables.description: Send a warning when disk usage reaches or exceeds this percentage.

variables.name: discord_webhook_url
variables.title: Discord Webhook URL
variables.description: Optional Discord webhook endpoint for warning notifications.

variables.name: discord_username
variables.title: Discord Username
variables.description: Sender name used for Discord webhook messages.
